description: UE5 splines: USplineComponent, USplineMeshComponent, paths, roads, rails, procedural placement along curves

entries.title: Spline Components
entries.description: USplineComponent defines a curve in 3D space with editable points. Used for roads, rivers, roller coasters, cable paths, AI patrol routes, and procedural mesh placement along curves.
entries.whatItIs: USplineComponent is a SceneComponent that stores a Catmull-Rom or Bezier curve defined by control points. It provides functions to query positions, directions, and distances along the curve, making it the standard tool for defining paths and placing objects along curves.
whenToUse: Defining paths for actors to follow — AI patrol routes, moving platforms, rail systems
Procedurally placing meshes along a curve — fences, roads, cables, pipes
Creating rivers, roads, or tracks that artists can edit by dragging points in the editor
Any system that needs a designer-editable curve in 3D space
whenNotToUse: Simple point A to point B movement — just Lerp between two points
NavMesh-based AI pathfinding — use the navigation system instead
Animation paths — use Sequencer or root motion
commonMistakes: Not specifying ESplineCoordinateSpace (Local vs World) when querying positions — gets wrong results when the actor is transformed
Using GetLocationAtSplinePoint only (integer index) instead of GetLocationAtDistanceAlongSpline (smooth interpolation) for movement
Not closing the spline loop when it should loop — set bClosedLoop on the component
Creating USplineMeshComponents in BeginPlay without setting their mobility to Movable — static meshes can't be modified at runtime
Placing too many spline points when fewer would produce the same curve — more points = more computation
keyPoints: GetLocationAtDistanceAlongSpline(Distance, Space): world position at a distance along the curve
GetRotationAtDistanceAlongSpline(): rotation (tangent direction) at that distance
GetSplineLength(): total length of the curve in world units
GetNumberOfSplinePoints(): how many control points the spline has
GetLocationAtSplinePoint(Index, Space): position at a specific control point
USplineMeshComponent: deforms a static mesh to follow a spline segment — used for roads, pipes, cables
bClosedLoop: if true, the spline connects end to start
Edit in editor: select the spline, drag points, right-click to add/remove points
